title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot discusses his investigative methodology with Inspectors Cargraves and Colgate, explaining that he focuses on identifying patterns within expert crimes. He highlights a specific similarity between the death of Alice Corey and the case of Nellie Parsons: in both instances, the husbands involved possessed airtight alibis.

Colonel Weston informs Poirot of his decision to transfer the investigation to Scotland Yard. Weston believes the case is linked to drug smuggling at Elfin Bay involving Horace Blatt. He theorizes that Blatt uses a boat with a red sail to transport drugs, potentially hiding them in the Fairy Cave on a rocky outcropping. Weston suggests the drugs might be exchanged via a sandwich box placed in a lady's swimming bag during lunch, and posits that Elena Marshall was killed because she witnessed Blatt hiding goods in the cave. While Poirot expresses doubt regarding the certainty of proving Blatt's guilt, he decides to take action by arranging a picnic.

To lift the spirits of the guests, Poirot persuades a group to join an outing to Dartmoor. The group includes Emily Bryce, the Redferns, the Stephens-Lennon-Gardners, Rosamond Darnley, Linda Marshall, and Mr. Bratt. Major Barry refuses to attend. Although Rosamond Darnley initially intends to stay behind because Linda has a headache, Mr. Burt and Mr. Bratt insist she join the group. The party departs in three cars, with the Gardners and Poirot traveling together.
